Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses And Strike Improper Objections By Sager To Special Interrogatories (Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Sager And His Attorney ; Plaintiff Conrad'S Rule 335 Statement In Support
Off Calendar Apr-20-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Sherri Conrad, Plaintiff/Moving party, appeared in Propria Persona
Patricia Kennedy Fyfe, Esq., for Defendant/Opposing party
Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted with modification to amount of sanctions. Plaintiff Sherri Conrad's Motion To Compel Further Responses And To Strike Improper Objections To Special Interrogatories is granted in part and denied in part.
The general and specific objections are overruled and ordered stricken as they assert that discovery is ongoing and as they reserve a right to supplement responses. There is no attorney-client or attorney work product objection which applies to interrogatories, except as noted in this order. Objections of harassment are overruled and ordered stricken. Responses which reference other documents and answers which refer to previous answers are inadequate, and that language is ordered stricken.
According to the reply, certain interrogatories are no longer at issue as Defendant has agreed to respond further. As to the remaining interrogatories in dispute, Defendant is ordered to respond further except as to the following:
Defendant is ordered to respond further to Nos. 16-18, limited to non-attorneys. The motion is denied as to Nos. 19-24 as it invades the attorney-client privilege.
Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order.
The court finds that the responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded in the amount of $1103.63, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
